Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

(9 comments)

1) I don't see the need to add a cl_object_ioctl() method here when adding cl_object_getstripe() would be fine and add less indirection. But I will not hold up this change for that.

2) As a part of LSM encapsulation I was planning on killing the terrible function obd_packmd(). But again I will not hold up this change for that.

Patch Set 13:

But I think this discussion lends further weight to the argument that we should not be using ioctls for these operations.

Patch Set 13: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

I am not particularly comfortable with the idea of introducing ioctl structures as an API to Lustre internals.  The ioctl should be handled closer to the boundary between the kernel and user-space, and then internally lustre should use reasonable abstractions and data accessing functions of its own.

commit.subject: LU-5823 clio: add coo_getstripe interface
commit.message:
LU-5823 clio: add coo_getstripe interface

Use cl_object_operations::coo_getstripe() to handle
LL_IOC_LOV_GETSTRIPE ops.

Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam.xu@intel.com>
Change-Id: Iee0d007f773ead35434eb88e7ca2f31c0bc42c11
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/12452
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
